paper_title: Geochemical and Sedimentary Analysis of Lake Ledro Cores

methods.description: Analysis of the chemical composition of sediment core LL08-1 using an XRF core scanner and ICP-AES to determine elemental concentrations.

steps.description: The chemical composition of sediment core LL08-1 was analysed using an Avaatech XRF core scanner at a resolution of 0.2 mm and 2 mm.
steps.searchable_summary: analyze sediment core using XRF core scanner
outputs: XRF data for Si, Ca, K, Ti, Zr

steps.description: XRF measurements were carried out on split cores with a measurement duration of 20 s using specific voltage and current settings for different elements.
steps.searchable_summary: perform XRF measurements with specified settings

steps.description: 49 samples from the laminated facies of LL08-1 were processed for ICP-AES measurements.
Approximately 80 mg of sediment were fully dissolved under pressure at 100°C using a mixture of suprapure grade HCl, HNO3, and HF.
steps.searchable_summary: dissolve sediment samples using acid mixture

methods.description: Chronology establishment using AMS 14C ages and 137Cs measurements to create an age-depth model.

steps.description: 19 AMS 14C ages measured on terrestrial plant macrofossils and 137Cs measurements taken from the topmost 32 cm of the sediment record.
steps.searchable_summary: measure AMS 14C ages and 137Cs in sediment

steps.description: All radiocarbon ages were calibrated using the program Calib 6.06 with the Intcal09 calibration set.
Results from radiocarbon and short-lived radio-isotope analyses were combined to produce an age-depth model using a smooth cubic spline model in 'Clam' software.
steps.searchable_summary: create age-depth model using Clam software

methods.method_name: Flood Deposits Counting and Frequency Analysis
methods.description: Automatic and naked-eye counting of flood deposits and frequency analysis using RGB scale and Gaussian mixture model.

steps.description: Core-image treatment by solarisation to identify flood deposits using RGB scale.
Normalise the 1/Red signal by removing the low-frequency trend using a moving first quartile.
steps.searchable_summary: normalize 1/Red signal by removing low-frequency trend

steps.description: Use a Gaussian mixture model to decompose the peak component and choose the threshold value.
steps.searchable_summary: apply Gaussian mixture model to peak component

steps.description: Evaluate the distribution of peaks along the sequence using a smoothing sum of episodes with a moving time window.
steps.searchable_summary: evaluate peak distribution with smoothing sum

steps.description: Naked-eye counting of the dark-brown flood deposits performed three times by different persons and results averaged.
steps.searchable_summary: perform naked-eye counting of flood deposits
